name: Eothraim
description: Semi-nomadic horse-people — superb riders, herders and hunters who follow their flocks between seasonal pastures. Proud and honour-bound, though split into many independent groups. Toward travellers: wary but hospitable to those who show respect; quick to take offence at insult or trespass.

name: Elven Noble
description: A high-born Elf of one of the great houses, travelling with attendants on errands of state, kinship or lore, bearing authority lightly but unmistakably. Toward travellers: gracious and dignified but remote; a meeting feels like an audience as much as an encounter.

name: Common Folk
description: Ordinary working people of town and country — farmers, herders, craftsfolk and labourers going about daily life. Toward travellers: they want mainly to be left in peace, and their welcome to strangers swings from kindness to wariness.

name: Dwarves of the Iron Hills
description: Durin's Folk who mine and smelt the rich iron of the Iron Hills east of Mirkwood — renowned smiths of weapons and armour, trading with the Men of Dale and the Lake. Toward travellers: proud, industrious and stout in war; wary of strangers but fair to honest dealers and staunch allies.

name: Laiquendi (Green-elves)
description: The Green-elves of Ossiriand — a reclusive woodland Elf-folk of the Elder Days, deeply bound to forest and river. Toward travellers: secretive and seldom seen, keeping to their woods; wary of strangers but not hostile without cause.

name: Asdriags
description: A savage, wain-riding Easterling people who roam the grasslands in search of plunder. Toward travellers: hostile and predatory — raiders who fall upon the unwary rather than parley.

name: Daen folk
description: The native pre-Númenórean people of the coasts and plains — shorter and stockier than the Dúnedain — who took up Westron and Númenórean ways while keeping their old songs and lore; they make up most of the common folk of Lebennin and Anfalas. Toward travellers: settled, ordinary country people whose welcome ranges from hospitable to wary.

name: Daen Coentis
description: Mountain clans of the White Mountains living above the treeline in simple groupings — semi-nomadic goat-herds and, on the coast, fisherfolk — who have largely shunned Gondorian ways and keep their ancient traditions. Toward travellers: insular and proud, suspicious of outsiders but not hostile without cause.

name: Galadhrim (Elves of Lórien)
description: The Silvan and Sindarin Elves of Lórien, masters of woodcraft and stealth, dwelling in flets high among the great mallorn-trees. Toward travellers: secretive guardians of their golden wood, unseen until they choose; courteous but exacting toward strangers who enter uninvited.

name: Sakalai
description: Small, dark-haired, independent tribes of the southern marshes and the mouths of Anduin, of mixed Daen and Southron blood, living as swamp-fishers largely beyond Gondor's reach. Toward travellers: keep to themselves in their fens, wary of outsiders and of any authority.

name: Elves of Rivendell
description: The mixed Elf-folk of Rivendell — Noldor lords and craftsmen, Sindar, and many Silvan Elves who tend its grounds and patrols — tall, ageless and hardy, gathered in willing service around the Lord of the House. Toward travellers: gracious hosts within their hidden valley, giving rest, healing and counsel to the worthy, and ready to defend it at need.

name: Elrond
description: Elrond Half-elven, lord and founder of Rivendell — descended from both Elves and Men, keeper of Arnor's heirlooms and bearer of one of the Three Rings; a healer, loremaster and host of the wise. Toward travellers: a noble, kindly and far-sighted lord, a giver of shelter, healing and counsel to those who reach his house in need.

name: Galadriel
description: Lady of Lórien — a great Noldorin princess, Ring-bearer, and one of the wisest and most far-seeing of Elves, whose foresight and power guard her realm against the Shadow. Toward travellers: regal, ancient and searching; gracious to the worthy who reach her wood, but her gaze reads the heart.

name: Hillmen of Rhudaur
description: Short, dark, stocky hill-folk of broken Rhudaur, kin to the Dunlendings — superb mountaineers but deeply distrustful of outsiders and prone to treachery and ambush, worshipping vengeful gods with blood; old enemies of Dúnedain and Northmen, and dreading Elves. Toward travellers: dangerous and faithless to strangers, quick to ambush and slow to trust.

name: Calenardhons
description: The Men of the grassy plains south of Fangorn — herders and settlers along the Entwash, forerunners of the Rohirrim — who graze and trade near the forest and keep an uneasy peace with its powers. Toward travellers: plain herding folk, cautious but not hostile, ever watchful of the forest at their backs.

name: Rural Men
description: A broad label for the common country folk of Eriador and Gondor — of mixed blood, plainly dressed, working farm, pasture and trade. Toward travellers: ordinary working people whose welcome to strangers ranges from kindly to wary with the times.

name: Woodmen
description: Northmen who dwell in and beneath the trees of Mirkwood as loose clans of hunters and gatherers — fair, strong and bearded, and expert climbers at home in the branches. Toward travellers: self-reliant forest folk, wary of strangers but fair to those who respect the woods, and foes of the forest's evils.

name: Thranduil's Silvan elves
description: The Wood-elves of northern Mirkwood, dwelling in caverns and halls under their king — lovers of feasting, song and tongues, keeping to themselves and largely indifferent to Men and Dwarves unless threatened. Toward travellers: merry among their own but guarded with outsiders, suspicious of strangers in their woods and quick to detain trespassers.

name: Beornings
description: A scattered folk of huge, immensely strong men akin to the Woodmen — exceedingly hairy and fair-skinned herders, bee-keepers and woodsmen of the Anduin vales, some said to take bear-shape. Toward travellers: independent and hospitable to friends but fierce to foes, keeping the river-lands clear of orcs and wolves.

name: Gondorian farmers
description: The farming folk of Gondor's heartland vales — Anórien, Ithilien and Lebennin — of mixed Númenórean and local blood, working fields and pastures and supplying the great roads and the river. Toward travellers: settled, ordinary country people, generally peaceable and used to traffic on the roads.

name: Gramuz
description: Brave, hardy foresters and horse-herders with strong warrior traditions. Toward travellers: tough but honourable folk, wary of strangers though not aggressors.

name: Sagath
description: Nomadic horsemen and chariot-riders who range the open pastures. Toward travellers: a warlike steppe people, raiders quick to fall upon the unwary.

name: Variags
description: A brutal, semi-nomadic people of Khand, southeast of Mordor — very dark-skinned, black-haired and red-eyed, tireless horsemen long swayed by Mordor and hardened by constant war. Toward travellers: hostile and warlike, allies of the Enemy — dangerous to meet on their lands or in war.

name: Wandering Eothraim
description: Semi-nomadic herders and horse-masters who roam the pastures, clashing with Easterling tribes over grazing-land. Toward travellers: proud horse-folk, wary but hospitable to those who respect them, and hard on trespassers.

name: Rohirrim Messengers
description: Riders and military columns of Rohan and Gondor moving along the Great West Road — couriers, troops and supply trains, especially in time of war. Toward travellers: purposeful and in haste; no threat to honest folk, but they will question strangers near the war-roads.

name: Urban Northmen
description: Merchants and settlers of the Celduin valley who came to share in eastern trade — town-dwellers of fortified places, close to Gondor in culture but proud of their Northman roots. Toward travellers: trade-minded townsfolk, generally welcoming to honest dealers.

name: Bandits and deserters
description: Disaffected soldiers, desperate peasants and local rogues haunting the roads and ruins of the depopulated frontier, caring for nothing but what they can steal. Toward travellers: a direct danger — robber bands that prey on the lone and the laden.

name: Sea lords of Lebennin
description: The High Men of Gondor's coast, whose power is rooted in Pelargir and the seaward towns — commanders of fleets and rulers of the province, leading the long fight against Corsairs and Haradan raiders. Toward travellers: proud, authoritative lords of the sea-coast, correct with strangers and watchful for enemies from the water.

name: Gondorian Quarrymen
description: Gondorian labourers who work the ancient stone quarries on the forest's northern margins. Toward travellers: ordinary working men, no threat — a sign of organized Gondorian industry nearby.

name: Dwarves of Erebor
description: Durin's Folk of the Lonely Mountain, using its caverns as halls, mines and treasure-vaults and bound tightly to the trade of the River Running. Toward travellers: proud, industrious and rich, wary of strangers but fair to honest dealers and staunch in alliance.

name: Marshmen
description: Small, long-lived descendants of the Edain dwelling in the western marshes, living by hunting, fishing, goat-herding and gardening and governed by yearly councils on a holy island; some driven west by the marshes' corruption. Toward travellers: a reclusive, peaceable fen-folk, keeping to their waters and wary of outsiders.

name: Eraguk
description: Fierce steppe nomads of mixed southern blood, the old inhabitants of the Aegardh, now mostly driven east or scattered into small bandit-like groups by Gondorian pressure. Toward travellers: a displaced, hard-pressed nomad folk, some reduced to raiding to survive.

name: Haruze
description: Farmers and nomads of the Vale of Harnen, growing sorghum and herding flocks and moving seasonally between Harad and the southern plateaus — a lasting presence even where Gondor claims rule. Toward travellers: southern country folk of contested borderlands, their welcome shifting with loyalties and the season.

name: Gondorian rangers
description: Border-wardens of Gondor who hold its southern fortresses and patrol the desert roads, keeping the contested marches nominally in hand — thin on the ground and often harried by nomads and enemy agents. Toward travellers: vigilant, hard-pressed king's men; allies to the lawful, sharp with the suspicious.

name: Umbarean agents
description: Captains and intriguers of Umbar who stir the nomad clans against Gondor, using the southern borderlands as a staging-ground and the trade-roads as their target. Toward travellers: covert and dangerous — agitators and spies who work in the shadows against Gondor.

name: Tharbad people
description: The crowded folk of the fen-ringed river-city of Tharbad — merchants, craftsmen, guildsmen, smugglers and refugees — in a town whose rule has slipped from kings to guilds as flood, war and plague wore it down. Toward travellers: a mixed, bustling, sometimes lawless river-city people; opportunity and risk both, depending whom you meet.

name: Rangers of Ithilien
description: Gondorian rangers who wage a hidden war in Ithilien — stealthy raiders who ambush Sauron's troops from cover and melt back into the wild. Toward travellers: watchful and unseen until they choose; allies to the free, but they challenge strangers in their secret country.

name: Estaravi
description: Northmen of the Great River valleys; some of their young warriors take service with the Witch-king's hosts, seeking wealth and glory. Toward travellers: ordinary river-vale folk, though some of their fighting men ride with the Enemy — loyalties to weigh with care.

name: Princes of Dol Amroth
description: The hereditary Dúnedain lords of Dor-en-Ernil, ranking just below King and Steward — a line carrying Elven blood, ruling almost as their own masters from Dol Amroth under the silver Swan-ship banner. Toward travellers: noble, gracious and high-bred lords of the sea-coast, courteous to the worthy and commanding in bearing.

name: Eredrim
description: Dunlending-kin highlanders of the Ered Tarthonion, descended from the mountain Daen — rugged village folk under their own chieftains, paying tribute in kind, keeping animist and sometimes dark rites and resisting outside ways. Toward travellers: insular and wary of strangers, clinging to old customs; suspicious, but not always hostile.

name: Elves of Edhellond
description: A Sindar-led Elf-haven at the mouth of the Morthond, famed for white swan-ships and as guardians of a sacred wood — Sindar shipwrights and captains over Silvan hunters and fishers, building the fairest ships of southern Gondor. Toward travellers: courteous sea-elves of a hidden haven, gracious to friends and to the worthy.

name: Celeborn
description: Lord of Lórien and consort of Galadriel — a wise Sindarin lord who gives the Golden Wood its military and diplomatic leadership and embodies its ancient traditions. Toward travellers: stately and reserved, courteous to the worthy who enter his realm but exacting of any who come uninvited.

name: Stoors of New Maresh
description: A small community of Stoor hobbits living in burrows on marsh-hills, travelling by pole-boat and trading herbs and fish, shielded by treacherous wetlands and their own wariness. Toward travellers: shy, peaceable marsh-hobbits, slow to trust outsiders but no threat.

name: Faithful of Arnor
description: The devout of Arnor who keep monastic houses, hermitages and small enclaves on hilltops and in defiles, joining contemplative life with simple gardening and herding. Toward travellers: peaceable, retiring religious folk, hospitable to the respectful and keeping to their quiet retreats.

name: Merchants
description: Traders moving goods between clans and to the great towns; some fronts hide a smugglers' trade in herbs grown for the Corsairs of Umbar. Toward travellers: generally welcoming dealers and a source of goods and news — though a few serve darker masters behind a trader's face.

name: Gondorian settlers
description: Gondorians who garrison the forts and farm the pockets of the eastern marches, guarding the fords and asserting Gondor's claimed dominion. Toward travellers: settled frontier folk and soldiers; correct with the lawful, watchful of strangers near the borders.

name: Smugglers
description: Privateers, pirates and smugglers working from coves, swamps and hidden harbours along the coasts, raiding trade and dodging the king's duties. Toward travellers: lawless and dangerous — they prey on shipping and trust no one they cannot use.

name: Dwarves of Nan-i-Naugrim
description: Firebeards and Broadbeams of the Blue Mountains, dwelling in the fourteen holds of their valley and keeping vigorous traditions of smithing and weapon-craft. Toward travellers: skilled, proud mountain-Dwarves; reserved with outsiders but renowned for their work and fair in trade.

name: Elves of Mithlond
description: The High Elves of Círdan's Grey Havens, who keep the western tower and road and crew the grey ships that bear the Eldar over Sea into the West. Toward travellers: ancient, serene and bittersweet sea-elves; courteous but remote, their faces ever turned toward the West.

name: Daen Iontis
description: A militant Dunnish faction bitterly hating the Dúnedain, bound to a temple of harsh, sacrificial religion and pushing for war — a dark twisting of the old Daen beliefs. Toward travellers: fanatical and hostile, above all to anyone of Dúnedain blood; dangerous zealots.

name: Angmar veterans
description: Former soldiers of Angmar settled as freemen in fortified border villages, who farm, patrol daily, escort supply-trains and turn out as troops in war. Toward travellers: hardened soldier-farmers, watchful and disciplined, wary of strangers on the marches.

name: Corsairs
description: Descendants of Gondorian rebels who fled to Umbar after the Kin-strife — strong-built, mostly fair Dúnedain stock, long warring with the southern peoples and burning to reclaim Gondor. Toward travellers: hostile sea-raiders and enemies of Gondor, pirates who plunder the coasts and take captives.

name: Dwarf Miners of Harlindon
description: Dwarves working the coal and iron mines on the eastern slopes of the southern Blue Mountains. Toward travellers: industrious, close-mouthed miners; reserved with strangers but no threat to honest folk.

name: Men of Rhudaur
description: Men of the fallen kingdom of Rhudaur, many of whom — land-hungry peasants especially — enlisted in Angmar's armies and later settled as soldier-farmers in fortified villages. Toward travellers: hardened border folk, some still bound to old Angmar loyalties; wary and not always friendly.

name: Hillmen shepherds
description: Hardy nomadic herders grazing their sheep through the southern valleys. Toward travellers: tough, self-sufficient hill-folk, guarded with strangers but rarely aggressors.

name: Rangers of the North
description: Dúnedain rangers who watch the wild northern lands and shadow the movements of the Witch-king's armies — secret guardians of the depopulated North. Toward travellers: grim, unseen protectors; they challenge strangers but defend the innocent and aid the worthy in need.

name: Rangers of Arthadan
description: Frontier shepherds and watch-forts of Arthedain's bleak northwestern marches, grazing the uplands and standing as staging-ground and militia against Angmar. Toward travellers: hard-pressed frontier folk and watchmen, wary on an exposed and war-torn border.

name: Lumimiehet
description: A wary snow-people who roam the northern wastes from spring to autumn, camping rough on rocky slopes for days at a time, reserved with strangers and apter to move on than to deal with rough folk. Toward travellers: shy and cautious, but approached gently they can give detailed knowledge of the land, its hazards and its ways.

name: Tinkers and travellers
description: Tinkers and peddlers who move steading to steading along the roads, doing odd jobs, selling small goods and carrying news and gossip. Toward travellers: friendly and talkative, a ready source of local news and rumour, and welcome company on the road.

name: Rivermen traders
description: River-folk who ply the Baranduin and other northern rivers, claiming the waters as their own and carrying trade and word between the northern realms. Toward travellers: independent boat-folk, useful for passage and news, but proprietary about their rivers.

name: Beffraen
description: A primitive woodland folk akin to the Wild-men, dwelling in the dark forests of a remote peninsula — secretive, using treacherous woods and coasts to stay beyond Dúnadan rule and keep their own ways. Toward travellers: shy and elusive, shunning outsiders; wary in their woods but not aggressors unless threatened.

name: Celebrimbor
description: The master-smith who founded the great Elven craft-guild of Eregion — among the greatest smiths of his people and the maker of the Three Elven Rings. Toward travellers: a noble, brilliant craftsman-lord, gracious to the worthy and wholly absorbed in his art.

name: Rohirrim
description: The horse-lords of Rohan — tall, fair, blond and bearded Northmen who live much of the year in seasonal pasture-camps, herding, hunting and fishing; proud riders with old hatreds for Dunlendings and orcs. Toward travellers: hospitable to honest, respectful folk, but suspicious of strangers and swift to challenge intruders on their lands.

name: Saralainn
description: Rowdy, semi-independent clans of the Gwathló mouthlands, their culture a blend of Dunnish ways and refugee influences — providing both raiders and stubborn defenders. Toward travellers: unruly and unpredictable; some hospitable, some quick to rob, depending on the clan and the day.

name: Dunlendings Bards
description: Singers and lore-keepers of the Dunlending clans, carrying genealogies, feuds and grievances in memorized verse; welcome at any hearth, their praise or mockery can make or unmake a man among the clans. Toward travellers: protected by custom and no fighters, but a powerful voice in the clans — and a keen source of local news and feud.

name: Snow-elves (Lossidil)
description: Reclusive Snow-elves of the far polar wastes, keeping an enchanted ice-city and patrolling the frozen north, guarding an ancient radiance and rarely meddling in mortal affairs. Toward travellers: remote, seldom-seen guardians of the ice; aloof, but not hostile to those who mean no harm.

name: Iathrim and Laegrim
description: Sindar and Green-elves of Harlindon and southern Lindon — many of them survivors of drowned Beleriand — who shape the woodland culture of the western shores, some chafing under Noldorin rule. Toward travellers: woodland Elf-folk of the coast, courteous but reserved with outsiders.

name: Lindellim (Nandor)
description: Nandor Elves and northern Sindar houses of Lindon, holding parts of its forlands and shores — an old woodland Elven lineage among the western realms. Toward travellers: secretive forest-Elves, wary of strangers but not hostile without cause.

name: Angmarim Soldiers
description: The mortal soldiery of Angmar — hardened northern fighters in the Witch-king's service, garrisoning his black towers and marching on the Dúnedain; grim and disciplined. Toward travellers: hostile servants of the Iron Crown who hunt and fight all enemies of Angmar.

name: Helechoth
description: A herding-and-hunting sub-folk of the far north, working inland herds across the cold uplands and dealing with both the snow-peoples and Angmar's frontier forces. Toward travellers: hardy northern herders, wary and self-contained on a bleak, contested frontier.

name: Umli (Half-dwarves)
description: A short, stocky northern folk said to spring from an ancient union of Men and Dwarves — strong, ruddy, red-haired and bearded, supremely hardy in cold but ill-suited to heat, and long-lived. Toward travellers: tough, insular cold-country folk; gruff with strangers but no enemy to honest travellers.

name: Dwarves of Khazad-dum
description: Durin's Folk in their ancestral glory at Moria, trading ore and craft with the Elves of Eregion in a famed friendship of shared learning. Toward travellers: proud master-smiths and miners at the height of their power; reserved but fair, and staunch friends to allies.

name: Merënyetsënyt
description: A snow-people of the Bay of Forochel who live on drifting icebergs and frozen shores, hunting whales, seals and walrus — short, stocky, fair-haired and astonishingly cold-hardy, though many are lost to the sea. Toward travellers: hardy, isolated ice-hunters, reserved with outsiders but not hostile.

name: Noldorin mystics
description: An obscure order of Noldorin mystics keeping a hidden valley-haven in the far north — a refuge for the Free Peoples and a base of resistance against the Witch-king. Toward travellers: secretive, wise and benevolent; a hidden help to the hunted who can find them.

name: Dunlendings Healers
description: Wise-women and herb-keepers of the Dunlending clans, tending wounds and sickness with simples, charms and old craft — respected and a little feared, guarding remedies and rites older than Rohan. Toward travellers: a possible source of healing and lore, though wary of outsiders and protective of their secrets.

name: Woses
description: The Drúedain — an ancient, diminutive forest-people unmatched in wood-craft and woodland warfare, thought ugly and long persecuted by Men and Elves, surviving hidden in the deep woods. Toward travellers: deeply wary and secretive after ages of hunting; no aggressors, but they watch unseen and trust few — yet stalwart friends to those who earn it.

name: Broadbeam Dwarf Patrol
description: A patrol of Broadbeam Dwarves warding the roads and mine-approaches of the Blue Mountains — grim and heavily armed, guarding their holds against orcs and trespassers and keeping the old ways open. Toward travellers: stern, watchful guards, correct with honest folk but quick to challenge strangers near their gates.

name: Slaves
description: Captives and subjugated folk of many origins, worked under the lash to support a warrior elite — broken and desperate people. Toward travellers: pitiable more than threatening, but the desperate can turn dangerous, and where they toil their cruel masters are near.

name: Mithrim Noldor Patrol
description: A roving Noldor war-band of the Blue Mountains and northern Lindon, watching the passes of the Ered Luin for orcs out of the ruined North and escorting travellers between scattered Elven settlements. Toward travellers: vigilant Elven guards — protective of the free and a help to honest wayfarers, sharp toward anything that smells of the Enemy.

name: Falathrim (Sea-elves)
description: Sea-elves of the western coasts, masters of shipbuilding and sailing — fishers and net-menders in the river-mouths, shipwrights and captains in the havens, crews of the grey ships that ward the shores. Toward travellers: courteous coast-dwelling Elves, bound to sea and ship, reserved with strangers but not unkind.

name: Calenardhons Soldiers
description: Garrison troops and militia of Calenardhon — Gondor's grassland province before Rohan — holding its forts, fords and roads against orcs and Dunlending raiders out of the hills. Toward travellers: disciplined king's soldiers, allies to the lawful and watchful of strangers on a raided frontier.

name: Urban Men
description: A broad label for the town and city folk of Eriador and Gondor — the common people of places like Bree, Tharbad, Pelargir and Minas Tirith — of mixed blood and every trade. Toward travellers: ordinary townsfolk used to strangers and traffic, their welcome ranging from busy indifference to friendliness or suspicion.

name: Bree-landers
description: Mixed folk of Bree — Men of various stocks and hobbits together — living in and around a hedged, trench-ringed village at a great crossroads, once a busy trade-town and now somewhat faded. Toward travellers: used to strangers and the road; generally welcoming but cautious, with a homely, gossipy good sense.

name: Dorwinrim
description: The folk of Dorwinion by the Sea of Rhûn — of mixed Northman and Easterling blood, somewhat stocky and fair with a faint yellowish cast — a people of the wine-lands between the great realms. Toward travellers: traders and vintners of the eastern marches whose welcome depends on the dealings and the day.

name: Asdriag & Sagath Soldiers
description: Easterling warriors of the lands beyond Rhûn who ride under Sauron's banners as auxiliaries — horse-borne, lightly armed, fighting for plunder and dark lords' promises. Toward travellers: hostile raiders of the eastern marches, a danger to any of the free peoples they meet.

name: Beijabar of Nan Anduin
description: A Northman folk of the Anduin vales akin to the Beornings — bee-keepers, herders and woodsmen who hold the river-lands and keep them clear of orcs and wolves. Toward travellers: independent folk, hospitable to friends but fierce to foes.

name: Dunedain Traveller
description: A lone Dúnadan on the road — one of the long-lived, far-wandering Men of Westernesse, abroad on some errand of their own; grave, courteous and watchful. Toward travellers: a doughty, honourable companion who keeps their lineage and purpose close.

name: Falathrim Fisher Sailor
description: Sea-elves of the Lindon coast met at their daily craft — casting nets in the river-mouths, mending boats on the strand, working a small smack along the gulf — weathered and unhurried, knowing every current and tide. Toward travellers: calm, courteous coast-folk and a good source of passage and sea-lore for the respectful.

name: Dunlendings Priests
description: Keepers of the old Dunlending faith — leaders of rite and sacrifice who tend the clans' ancestral spirits at hill-shrines and standing stones, holding great sway and deep suspicion of outland gods and ways. Toward travellers: powerful and insular, hostile to outsiders who meddle with their rites or sacred places.

name: Dunlendings Warriors
description: Fierce clan-warriors of Dunland with spear, axe and round shield, nursing an old hatred of Rohan for lost lands, fighting in loose bands under their headmen and raiding the Westfold when they can. Toward travellers: dangerous and quick to violence, above all toward Rohirrim and their friends.

name: Dwarf Prospector
description: A lone Dwarf or small party searching the roots of the Blue Mountains for fresh seams of iron, coal or precious metal — secretive about finds, ranging the high valleys with pick, lamp and a keen eye for stone. Toward travellers: gruff and close-mouthed about their work, but no threat to honest folk.

name: Foreigners
description: Men from distant lands marked by strange dress, speech and custom — traders, envoys, mercenaries or wanderers far from home. Toward travellers: regarded with curiosity or suspicion, their intent unknown until shown — anywhere from friendly to dangerous.

name: Dyrians
description: Folk of Dyr in the eastern marches near the Sea of Rhûn — traders, herders and rivermen living between the great realms, prizing their wine, horses and hard-won independence. Toward travellers: proud, self-reliant border-folk; fair traders, wary of being ruled.

name: Farmer Woodsman
description: Settled country folk working a farm or stretch of woodland at the edge of the wild — tilling, herding, felling and charcoal-burning; hospitable but guarded, and the first to suffer when raiders or beasts come out of the wilderness. Toward travellers: kindly to honest folk and a source of food and shelter, but wary on an exposed frontier.

name: Dwarf Merchant
description: A Dwarven trader on the road with a laden pony or wagon, dealing in metalwork, tools and weapons between the Dwarf-holds and the towns of Men and Elves — a shrewd bargainer travelling well-guarded. Toward travellers: open to trade and news, canny and close with their accounts, but fair to honest dealers.

name: Hobbits
description: A rare meeting with hobbits beyond their usual haunts — small, soft-spoken folk easily overlooked, usually a family or two strayed from the settled lands. Toward travellers: shy of big folk and quick to slip from sight, wanting little more than to be left to their meals and pipes.

name: Fisherfolk
description: Folk who live by the water — netting fish, gathering shellfish along the shores, poling boats through the shallows — weathered, close-knit, and reading the water better than any map. Toward travellers: settled waterside folk, helpful for passage and food, keeping to their own stretch of bank or coast.

name: Cultirith Patrol
description: A patrol of soldiery from the stronghold of Cultirith, warding its district against raiders and brigands, keeping the roads and challenging strangers in their lord's lands. Toward travellers: lawful men-at-arms; no threat to the honest, but they will stop and question those who pass.

name: Hillmen
description: Rough hill-dwelling folk of the uplands — herders, hunters and raiders who answer to clan and feud rather than king, hardy and clannish, holding the high valleys softer folk avoid. Toward travellers: wary of lowlanders and quick to arms; their welcome depends on clan and circumstance.

name: Tribesmen
description: Folk of a clan or tribe beyond the settled realms — hillmen, marsh-dwellers, herders or hunters bound by kin and custom rather than king. Toward travellers: proud and wary of outsiders, peaceable if respected but fierce in defence of their own ground.

name: Gondor Garrison Patrol
description: Soldiers of a Gondorian garrison on their rounds — keeping the walls, roads and watch-posts, checking travellers and watching the borders; disciplined men of the South Kingdom. Toward travellers: courteous to the lawful and hard on the suspect; they will stop and question strangers.

name: Horsefolk Éored
description: A mounted troop of the horse-folk ranging the grass-plains in war or on patrol — swift, disciplined and deadly with spear and bow from the saddle, covering ground no footman can match. Toward travellers: formidable riders; no threat to honest folk, but they will ride down and question strangers on their lands.

name: Hobbit Wanderer
description: A lone hobbit far from home — an oddity among his kind, driven by restlessness, exile or some half-mad errand; footsore and out of place yet surprisingly hardy, turning up in the strangest corners of the world. Toward travellers: harmless and out of his depth, but good company and tougher than he looks.

name: Laegrim Ranger
description: A ranger of the Green-elves warding the eaves of the forest realms — a silent woodsman and deadly archer who patrols the wild marches, shadows intruders and keeps the old paths. Toward travellers: unseen until he chooses; a guardian who watches strangers closely and aids the worthy.

name: Merchants & Traders
description: Traders plying goods between towns and lands — cloth, metalwork, salt, wine and small luxuries — with a laden pony, pack or guarded wagon; shrewd, talkative, carrying news as readily as wares. Toward travellers: welcoming and useful for goods and news, canny in a bargain but no threat.

name: Minas Ithil Courier
description: A rider bearing messages to and from Minas Ithil — an honest courier of Gondor in the realm's good days, but a servant of the Nazgûl carrying foul word once the tower became Minas Morgul. Toward travellers: in good times a lawful messenger; in evil times something to avoid — its errand is dark.

name: Nobles & Leaders
description: A person of rank abroad with their retinue — a lord, lady, captain or chieftain on some errand of state, war or kinship, travelling attended and armed. Toward travellers: they expect deference and can prove a powerful friend or a perilous enemy; mind your manners.

name: Dwarves
description: A chance meeting with Dwarves on the road — usually a small party travelling between their mountain-holds and the markets of Men — blunt, watchful and quick to take offence or oath, keeping their own counsel. Toward travellers: gruff and guarded but honest, fair to fair dealing and staunch once a bond is made.

name: Scout Elves
description: Elven scouts ranging the wild country about Rivendell and the Trollshaws, watching the fords and old roads — moving unseen, marking the passage of orcs and brigands and carrying word swiftly home. Toward travellers: hidden guardians who observe strangers before showing themselves, and help the worthy in peril.

name: Sailors
description: Seafaring and river-going men met in port or aboard ship — crewing trade-vessels, fishing boats and ferries; hardy, superstitious and free with a tale, knowing the coasts, currents and harbours. Toward travellers: easy company and a source of passage and sea-lore, harmless ashore unless badly crossed.

name: Pirates
description: Sea-raiders who prey on coastal shipping and settlements, putting in to plunder and slipping away with their spoil — lawless and dangerous, sometimes in league with the Corsairs, sometimes for themselves. Toward travellers: a direct danger by sea and shore; they rob, take captives and kill.

name: Brigands & Outlaws
description: Lawless men of the road and wild — robbers, deserters, broken soldiers and hired ruffians who prey on travellers and lone farms, banding where order is weak and scattering before real force. Toward travellers: a direct danger; they demand toll or plunder at knife-point but break before a strong, armed party.

name: People Fur Trappers
description: Men who range the northern woods trapping beasts for pelts — solitary, hardy and weather-worn, coming to town only to sell furs and resupply before vanishing back into the trees. Toward travellers: gruff loners and no threat, a possible source of guidance and trade in the deep wilds.

name: People Hunters
description: Men who live by the chase — hunting game in forest, hill and fen for meat and hides, skilled with bow and snare and at home far from any settlement, reading sign townsfolk would miss. Toward travellers: self-reliant woodsfolk, helpful and knowledgeable, wary but rarely hostile.

name: People Reed Peat Cutters
description: Marsh-folk who cut reed for thatch and dig peat for fuel, working the bogs by flat-bottomed boat — poor and little regarded, but knowing the safe paths through mires that swallow the unwary. Toward travellers: humble fen-folk and a precious guide through treacherous marsh for those who treat them kindly.

name: Elves
description: A chance meeting with Elves abroad — usually a small party on the road: travellers between the havens, a company keeping to the dusk, or hunters far afield. Toward travellers: courteous but reserved with strangers, gracious to the worthy, and seldom lingering long.

name: Merchant Caravan
description: A train of merchants travelling together for safety — wagons, pack-beasts and hired guards strung along the trade-roads; a rich prize for brigands, and so rarely seen without an armed escort. Toward travellers: welcoming and a source of goods and news, well-guarded and wary of ambush.

name: Pilgrim Wanderer
description: A lone traveller on a journey of faith or penance, bound for some shrine or vow far from home — footsore and single-minded, trusting to charity and to the protection their purpose is thought to grant. Toward travellers: harmless and devout, glad of kindness and company and no threat.

name: Pilgrim to Mithlond
description: An Elf making the last journey west to the Grey Havens to take ship into the Undying Lands — passing quietly through Lindon, alone or in small companies, thoughts already on the sea and the weariness of ages plain upon them. Toward travellers: gentle and sorrowful, wholly turned westward; courteous but already half-departed from the world.

name: Plague Refugee
description: Folk fleeing pestilence — driven from a stricken town, carrying what little they could and perhaps the sickness itself; desperate and shunned, begging shelter and food. Toward travellers: pitiable but a real danger — the wary turn them away for fear of the plague they may carry.

name: Renegade Elf
description: An Elf who has forsaken his people — outlawed for some old deed, soured by grief, or gone solitary and strange in the wild — wary, unpredictable, and answering to no lord. Toward travellers: a wild card who may aid a stranger as readily as rob one, and not to be trusted on sight.

name: Sindar Guardian
description: A Sindarin warden set to keep some threshold of the Elven lands — a ford, a forest-eaves, a hidden path — silent and watchful, clad to melt into wood and shadow. Toward travellers: challenges intruders from hiding but speeds friends quietly on their way; a guardian, not a hunter.

name: Sea Captain Sailor
description: An Elven ship-master of Círdan's folk, commanding one of the grey vessels of the Gulf of Lhûn and the Harlindon coasts — long-sighted and sea-hardened, carrying messages, goods and passengers between the havens, and at the last those bound west. Toward travellers: courteous and capable, a means of passage and sea-lore for the worthy.

name: Scouts
description: Lightly-armed men ranging ahead of a larger force or watching a stretch of country — outriders, foresters and trackers in someone's service, keeping out of sight and carrying word swiftly back. Toward travellers: watchful and elusive; whether they help or hinder depends on whom they serve.

name: Rivermen
description: Men who live and work the great rivers — boatmen, ferriers, fishers and traders moving goods and folk by water, knowing every shoal and landing and keeping a shrewd independence from the land's lords. Toward travellers: useful for passage and river-lore, independent-minded but generally fair to honest custom.

name: Slaves and Guards
description: A coffle of captives driven under armed guard toward the markets or some master's labour, herded by hard-eyed keepers — the guards watchful and brutal, the slaves broken but alert for any chance to flee. Toward travellers: the guards are violent and suspicious; the slaves may beg aid or seize a chance to break free.

name: Southerners
description: Men of the southern lands — Haradrim and their neighbours, dark-haired and richly or strangely arrayed — met as traders, envoys, mercenaries or soldiers far north of home, often eyed with mistrust in the West. Toward travellers: their intent varies by errand and allegiance; treated with suspicion in northern lands, friend or foe by circumstance.

name: Thieves & Smugglers
description: Footpads, cutpurses and smugglers working the towns, fairs and trade-roads — moving stolen goods and contraband, slipping tolls and gates, and lightening the purses of the unwary. Toward travellers: quick-fingered and quick to vanish, a danger to your goods and gold rather than your life — but dangerous if cornered.

name: Spies
description: Agents on secret business — watching roads and garrisons, carrying word, or worming into the trust of the powerful — passing as travellers, merchants or servants. Toward travellers: outwardly harmless but dangerous in what they serve; a careless word to one may travel far.

name: Wandering Company
description: A band of folk travelling the roads together — players, pilgrims, displaced families or companions on a long road — sharing fire and the safety of numbers. Toward travellers: friendliness and fortunes vary, but a company on the move always carries news from afar and welcomes company on a lonely stretch.

name: Wild Hobbits
description: Hobbits living rough at the edges of settled country — in the eaves of old forests, hedges and lonely downs — warier and more weather-hardened than their kin, trusting no big folk and vanishing into bracken at the first alarm. Toward travellers: shy and elusive and no threat, gone from sight before you know they were there.

name: Solitary Dwarf
description: A single Dwarf met far from any hold — a wanderer, exile, craftsman seeking work, or one bent on a private errand or grudge; gruff with strangers but steadfast to those who earn it. Toward travellers: wary and close-mouthed but honest and stout-hearted, a good companion once trust is won.

name: Armed Patrol
description: A body of armed men watching the roads, borders and marches of a realm — soldiers, militia or a lord's riders — challenging strangers and hunting bandits and raiders. Toward travellers: their temper depends on whose land it is and how the times sit — lawful aid or stern suspicion.

name: Elven Wandering Company
description: One of the roving Elf-companies that cross the lands at dusk, singing as they go, keeping to the old paths between the havens and the eastern realms. Toward travellers: gracious and otherworldly; they share fire and counsel with the worthy and are gone by morning.

name: Travellers
description: Folk upon the road for their own reasons — bound for market or kin, fleeing hard times, or simply going from town to town — usually harmless and glad of company on a lonely stretch. Toward travellers: generally friendly fellow-wayfarers, though the road draws all sorts and not every one is what they seem.

name: Military Unit
description: An organized force of soldiery on the move — a company, column or picked band marching to muster, garrison or war under its captains; disciplined and well-armed. Toward travellers: no foe for a small party and brooking no interference — lawful or hostile by whose banner it bears.

name: Elven Loremaster
description: A wandering Elven sage — keeper of histories, songs and ancient craft — journeying between libraries, ruins and the houses of the Wise, who remembers Ages Men have wholly forgotten. Toward travellers: gracious and generous with knowledge to the courteous, a priceless source of lore and counsel.
